text:
Preface

I have great pleasure in presenting herewith Book II of Deva Keralam.

The encouragement given by the readers following appearance of Book I hereof is
thankfully acknowledged.

Although the original is popularly attributed to Achyuta, in actuality Venkatesa’s
name also appears as author at one stage. This Venkatesa need not be confused with
Sarvartha Chintamani’s author, vide notes given under sloka 1 infra. The original
authorship is actually divided as under:

Sloka No. 1 to 3407 by Achyuta in the first phase.

Sloka No. 1 to 4775 by Venkatesa in the later phase.

Thus there is a total of 8102 original slokas for which our arrangement of presentation
is as under:

Sloka 1 to sloka 2718 of Achyuta in Book I.

Sloka 2719 to sloka 3407 of Achyuta, and sloka 1 to sloka 1997 of Venkatesa in Book
0.

Rest of the work from slokas from No. 1998 to 4775 of Venkatesa is being brought
out as Book III.

In the present volumealso, there are many general principles applicable to the various
ascendants, apart from Nadiamsa discussions confined to given signs. The reader will be
able to understand the same as he progresses with the study of the text, remembering at the
same time the general outlines given in the preface for Book I.

Ihave every hope that the kind reader will forgive me for my shortcomings if any and
accord a favourable reception to my present efforts.

R. Santhanam

(Editor,

The Times Of Astrology)
9/1040 Govindpuri
Kalkaji

New Delhi 110 019
(India)

Tel 6462777

2719 - 27201/2, Trailokys Nadiamsa -
Capricorn Ascendant (Up to sloka 2769): (a) The
native of former half of Trailokya Nadiamsa in
Capricorn ascendant, in Mercury’s Trimsamsa, will

be the third issue of the family. His birth will be in the”

region of a great river, in a north-south spread street.
The house concerned will be in the eastern part (of the
town) and will be a north-facing one

(b) Find out in which Navamsa the 4th lord is
placed. The house of birth may (also) correspond to
the indications given by the said Rasi or one of its
trines.

Notes: (a) In Capricorn, Trailokya Nadiamsa
extends from 14° 48' to 15° 0,

Ignore Mercury’s Trimsamsa condition and fol-
low only the relevant Nadiamsa stipulation.

(b) This is a general rule and canbe applied to all
ascendants irrespective of the Nadiamsa, and is one
of the conditions stipulated for determining the home
of birth.

2721 - 27211/2. Prosperity: If the lord of the
ascendant is in the 2nd house as the 11th lord (Mars)
is inthe 4th house (in Aries), he will attain prosperity
after the thirtieth year of his age. This yoga will also
give him a physique which is free from diseases and
is strong,

Notes: The effects given apply to the whole of
Capricorn ascendant irrespective of the Nadiamsa as
could be seen below.

Prosperity: Saturnin the 2nd house in Aquarius,
while Mars is in the 4th house will assure prosperity

Deva Keralam 1

right from youth. The influence of Satur onthe 10th
house, Libra, is passed on through Mars. By himself,
Mars is a functional benefic for Capricorn. When he
is related to Saturn and the 10th house, the native of
Capricorn ascendant is sure to rise without obstacles.

Healthy Body: As the lord of the 11th house, 6th
from the 6th house, Mars has a definite say on health,
like Mercury the 6th lord. The association of Mars
with ascendant lord, Saturn, the latter placed in
Moolatrikona in the 2nd, is a plus point. Such a
person will be free from illness and will attain
prosperity.

Mercury in Aries, while Saturnis in the 2nd, will
also give similar effects for Capricorn natives. But
prosperity, though certain, will be through certain
odds caused by inimical activities as Mercury is also
the 6th lord, and gets his placement in a bitter
enemy’s sign. Certainty in prosperity is because
Mercury is the lord of the 9th for Capricorn and
bosom friend of Saturn.

2722 - 2723, Saturn in Pisces: Should Saturn be
found in Pisces, witha dual Navamsa, the subject will
be in the grip of penury during his childhood. He will
enjoy sound health and prosperity in his youth. After
25 years of age, goddess Lakshmi will bless his
family.

Notes : Yugmamsaka in this case is dual navamsa,
and notspecifically Gemini, as Pisces Rasiis referred
to. Saturn in Pisces will get one of the other three dual
Navamsa, viz. Virgo, Sagittarius and Pisces.

The hints given hereof apply to Capricorn as-
cendant, in general. By his placement in the 3rd
house, Saturn will aspect the 9th house, denoting
father. Wealth or poverty in childhood depends on
father (or mother). Saturn in Pisces with Virgo
Navamsa will be in the 9th from natal ascendant. In
Sagittarius Navamsa, he will be inthe 12th from natal
ascendant and in Pisces Navamsa, he will be in the
3rd in Vargottama. Saturn’s placement in Jupiter’s
sign or Navamsa will produce some adverse effects
about father, as Mercury, the lord of the 9th, does got

2 Chandra Kala Nadi

go well with Jupiter. Saturn in relation to the 9th from
a sign of Jupiter, for Capricorn, may in the initial
stage not give money through father, but is bound to
give good effects in the long run by virtue of his good
position and good rulership. For Saturn is a slow
planet in revealing good effects. As the lord of the
ascendant, he assumes the onus of protecting the 9th
house.

In my view, we should add Leo Navamsa as well
in which case father’s poverty will be intense.

In analysing a bhava vis a vis a planet’s place-
ment we better take into note two important factors:
The occupant’s relationship with the significator of
that bhava, on the one hand. On the other hand, the
relationship of the Rasi dispositor and Navamsa
dispositor of the occupant with a given significator.

Generally, Saturn related to the 9th is notso good
as he is quite allergic to the Sun, the significator of
father.

2724, Movable Ascendant: If birth be in a
movable ascendant in the subject Nadiamsa, in Mer-
cury’s Trimsamsa, the subject will be blood-red in
complexion. He will be of medium build with a
symmetric body.

Notes: See 2719 (a) supra regarding Trimsamsa
condition.

2725 - 27311/2. Capricorn Ascendant: (a) One
born in this ascendant, in the former halfof Trailokya
Nadiamsa, will be good-looking. intelligent, elo-
quent, be endowed with charming face and eyes, be
fortunate, religious and will enjoy effects of Raja
Yoga. He will be fond of superior ascetics and will
worship gods and Brahmins. he will eat limited food,
will speak within a limit and will sleep for limited
hours. He will conquer his Senses, and will be skillful
in understanding others’ thoughts. He will beautify
himself with ornaments and also be fond of orna-
ments. He will be an evil person to the evil and will
be unkind to unkind people. He will be bereft of
paternal fortunes (or happiness) and will have self-
earned wealth.

(b) Ifthe 4th lord (noted in Rasi diagram) is inthe
9th place in Navamsa (i.e 9th counted from Navamsa
ascendant), he will be skillful in law and philosophy.
The native of the former halfofthe subject Nadiamsa
(without reference to 4th lord above) will be helpful
to all, will be endowed with valour, adventure and
fame, be fond of listening to mythological stories, be
ever wealthy, well-disposed to the poor, will worship
Lord Siva by vows and fasts, and be devoted to Lords
Vishnu and Siva. He will ever take to virtuous path
and will have excellent religious attainments. He will
gain through agriculture, quadmpeds etc. He will
lord over villages and lands.

Notes: (a) Applicable only to the subject
Nadiamsa.

(b) Skill in law and philosophy applies to the 4th
lord’s position as specified in the text while the rest
applies to first half of Trailokya Nadiamsa with the
above mentioned placement of the lord of the 4th,
Mars.

For any ascendant, the 4th lord placed in the 9th
from the natal ascendant or from the Navamsa as-
cendant, will be good for legal and philosophical
attainments.

2732-27331/2. Co-born: (a) If the 3rd lord Jupi-
ter is in the 6th house from the ascendant Capricom,
ora trine thereof (i.c.2nd/10th), thesubject will have
neither an older co-born, nor a younger one.

(b) If the 2nd (Aquarius) is occupied by Saturn,
he will have a sister endowed with longevity, and
anotherasynonymof difficulties, but living ina good
abode, and yet anotherendowed withsons and daugh-
ter.

Notes: (a) For Capricorn ascendant, if Jupiter is
placed in Aquarius, Gemini, or Libra, one will be the
lonely child of the family.

(b) Obviously this position of Satu “enotes
three sisters, while Jupiter in 3rd altogether ceprives
one of co-born. We have no hints as to what Saturn
will do in this regard if he is placed in Gemini/Libra.
However, my suggestion is that Saturn as exalted
ascendant lord will normally not make the native a
lonely issue, nor wil] he do so while in Gemini, being
in direct aspect to the 3rd.

2734 - 2735. Mars: Should the 4th lord Mars be
conjunct Saturn or be in aspect to Saturn, the native
of the subject Nadiamsa will lose his mother in his
very childhood. If Mars be with the Sun in Aries, the
latter in his sign of exaltation, the native will be
wealthy with elephants.

Notes: Mars in the 4th with Saturn’s connection
is bad for mother’s life-span, only when the ascend-
ing degree in Capricorn is Trailokya Nadiamsa.
Otherwise in general for this ascendant, Mars placed
in the 4th with Saturn’s connection cannot cause
short-life for the mother of the native.

Mars and the Sunin Aries is a yoga for wealthand

Deva Keralam 3

prosperity for Capricorn natives in general.

2736. Mars-Saturn-Moon: If these three plan-
ets conjoin in the 9th or 10th house, the native will be
a skillful person. He will gain conveyances, be a poet,
will hold the assemblage of men in charm and be
endowed with four kinds of traditional conveyances
(viz. chariots, horses, elephants and men-borne
palanquins).

Notes: The conjunction referred to here should
occur in Virgo/Libra, and is of three angular 10105. It
will be far superior for a Capricom native occurring
in the 10th, the best of the angles for that matter,
where Saturn’s exaltation will add further weightage.
Asa result, the subject will receive the above effects
in an increased manner.

This yoga isalso applicable to Capricom ascend-
ant in general as it is conjunction of lords of the 1st,
4th and the 7th in the 9th or the 10th. But for the
Nadiamsa, poetic skills may not be so evident in the
10th house, Libra. Inthe 9th house, apart from giving
the results mentioned in the original, poetic skills
may be abundant as the Moon, signifying imagina-
tion, occupies Virgo, a fertile sign of intellect, and the
3 planets aspect the 3rd house, ruled by Jupiter.

"Poetic skills" denoted need not necessarily
confine to poetry. The 3rd house denotes one’s
ability as a writer and even as a singer.

2737, Jupiter in Pushkaramsa; The native of
Trailokya Nadiamsa in Capricorn having the planet
Jupiter in Pushkaramsa will enjoy riches right from
birth till the end.

Notes: See notes under sloka No.2699, page 239,
Book 1. Jupiter’s sign position is not prescribed in the
current sloka. Hence he can be in any welcome sign,
but with Pushkara Navamsa.

4 Chandra Kala Nadi

2738 - 2739. Marriage: During the rulership of
the 2nd dasa, when Jupiter transits a trine counted
from the sign occupied by 10th lord (Venus), the
native’s marriage will take place with a girl hailing
from the southern or south-eastern part of his birth
place. These results have elaborately been narrated in
Sukra Nadi in the chapter devoted to the Bhava
concerned, i.e. 7th house.

Notes: Although the text qualifies Venus by his
10th lordship, he is all-importnat in marriage as be is
the significator in principle. For any ascendant,
Jupiter’s trina] transit is essential with reference to
the following:

a) Venus. b) 7th lord from ascendant. c) the
Moon. d) the 7th lord from the Moon.

If Jupiter transit trinally with reference to more
than one of the above four, marriage will be expe-
dited.

There are other factors like Navamsa ascendant
involving Jupiter’s transit. But the above is the
beginning point of the probe.

The girl hailing froma given direction is peculiar
to the Nadiamsa in question.

2740. Single Marriage: If the 7th lord Moon is
ina fixed sign with a fixed Navamsa (not necessarily
Vargothama), while the significator (Venus) is also
in a fixed sign, it is said, there will be only one
marriage to the native of the subject Nadiamsa.

Notes: This clue about the native remaining with
one marriage nullifying other combinations for a
remarriage can be applied in respect of any ascend-
ant. Venus, the Moon, 7th lord and ascendant lord
being in fixed sign and fixed Navamsa disallow a
remarriage. This however does not define the quality
and length of first marriage.

2741 - 27431/2, Other Effects: (a) If the 7th lord
Moon (counted from natal ascendant) is in the 8th
house in Navamsa (‘.e. inthe 8th place from Navamisa
ascendant), he will be sinful by joining others’
women.

(b) One born in the former half of Trailokya
Nadiamsa in Capricom ascendant, having Venus in
Somavathi Nadiamsa will have secretive lust for
sexual pleasures. He will be so sexually excited as to
join his spouse.even on prohibited days.

(c) If Venus is in Kanthaa Nadiamsa, he will be
full of merry during sexual union.

(0) Ifthe 10th lord (Venus) is in the 4th (Aries).
the subject of the said Kanthaa Nadiamsa will per-
form good deeds and will have good conduct.

Notes; (a) The 7th lord from any natal ascendant
occupying the 8th place from Navamsa ascendant
will show want of morality, more on the part of the
spouse. The text takes specifically the Moon into
account as she is the 7th lord fro Capricorn.

(b) Prohibited days for sexual union, by Satsras
in gencral are: 1. Ekadasi (11th lunar day) in cither
fortnight.

2. Amavasya (30th lunar day).

3. Four days from the commencement of a
woman’s periods.

There are many more stipulations. The above is
a brief account.

(c) Kanthaa Nadiamsa occurs as under:

Movable signs 06° 00' to 06° 12'

Fixed signs 237 48' to 24° 00!

Common signs 21° 00' to 21° 12'

2744 - 2747. Third Dasa: See in which Navamsa
the 4th lord is placed. When Jupiter comes to the Rasi
idenical with that Navamsa, ora trine thereof, during
the currency of the dasa thatis the third from birth, the
native will get married. The second half of third dasa
will cause great happiness. He will befriend the king.
Goddess Lakshmi will shower Her mercy on his
family. After the age of 32, his prosperity will rise. He
will acquire wealth through Brahmins. In Ketu’s sub
period in the end of 3rd dasa, his country’s ruler will
be destroyed (?) and in the periods of the 9th lord, due
to change of ruler, comforts will prevail (?)

Notes: The 4th lord’s Navamsa position comes
in the picture only when Venus is in Aries in the 4th
house as stated in para “d” of slokas 2741 - 2743 1/
2 above in respect of timing marriage.

2748 - 2749, Irrelevant; When Saturn arrives in
Scorpio, the king will be involved in quarrels. With
debility of Venus, the sub period of Jupiter will
promote quarrels in Pandya kingdom. The kinsfolk
of the Pandya king will incur difficulties, will lose
their honour and lives. The king of Mlechcha will be
destroyed when Jupiter travels in the sign occupied
by 10th lord.

Deva Keralam 5

2750 - 2752. Trailokya Nadiamsa - Capricorn
Ascendant; The subject will beget a daughter when
Jupiter transits the 8th house (Leo). After the age of
35, his prosperity will be onthe rise and will cause the
mercy of goddess Lakshmi. Either his 39 year, or38th
year, will endanger his wife but she will recover
health early. The subject will obtain rulership, hap-
piness to his men and increase of wealth and grains.
Sages say, the dasa of Venus, who is in aspect to a
benefic, will beget him the mercy of goddess Lakshmi
and royal kind of comforts.

Notes: The dasa of Venus related to a natural
benefic being able to confer good effects can apply to
Capricorn in general. Such dasa will cause wealth,
including properties, comforts, honour, name, fame
etc.

Also see notes under slokas 2753 - 2757 relating
to Venus dasa.

2753 - 2757. Capricorn ascendant - Dasa Ef-
fects: (a) The second half 10th Jord’s dasa will cause

great happiness, enmity with army chief, and destruc-
tion of relatives of the native.

strife (?) If the 8th lord is exalted and be in the
company of the 4th lord Mars, the subject will be
endowed with full life-span. The sixth dasa will then
prove fatal.

Notes: In this context the 4th lord Mars features
because he is the exaltant dispositor of 8th lord. Their
conjunction in Aries where one is in exaltation and
another in own sign will prove helpful for lifespan.
Otherwise their conjunction should be carefully ap-
proached as it could cause fatal end if not short-life.

2765. Raja Yoga: The 9th lord exalted and
joining the significator (Jupiter - the significator for
Dhamma ruled by the 9th house, as the reference to
here is Dharma) along with a friendly planet. The
sign in question should not be an evil house, nor
inimical (to companions). He could be also conjunct
oraspected by another benefic or his dispositor. Then
the subject will construct many villages, tanks, tem-
ple-towers, towns and parks. He will be in the com-
pany of virtuous men and will win the hearts of
superior kings.

Notes: The sloka has two expressions which are
not relevant and which affect the continuity of the
relevantand stipulated conditions. These are: Mitrasya
cha uchche stithe = posited in a sign that exalts the
friend of, here, Venus or the sign Aries which exalts
the Sun (Mitra). Swaamina means the lord. These are
omitted in translation.

The essence of the yoga thus is, for Capricorn
ascendant, the conjunction of Venus with Jupiter
supported by another benefic viz. Mercury or a
friendly planet in a good house is a powerful Raja
yoga as a result of which the native will construct
villages & C. That is, he will render social services,
will be inthe company of good menand bea recipient
of royal or governmental favours.

Deva Keralam

2766 - 27671/2. Death: In the sub period of 10th
lord (Venus) during the operation of the fifth dasa, the
native of Trailokya Nadiamsa, in Capricorn ascend-
ant, will have his timely death. Ifat all he survives due
to merits (of his past birth), death will certainly strike
in the 68th year. The month concerned (In the said
year of death) will coincide with the Sun’s transit in
the three signs commencing from Taurus, or with the
Sun in Capricorn. The final moment will be in the
early hours of Friday.

2768 - 2769. He would have been a Brahmin in
his previous birth. His next birth will also be in a
Vaidik Brahmin’s family. He will attain emancipa-
tion after the next birth if Jupiter aspects the 12th (for
the Trailokya Nadiamsa native being discussed).

Notes: The following two lines out of tke above
four lines have not been provided with translation as
a part of the text.

Randradhipa dasa kaale gadaamse viprakaalage
which means “For native of Gada Nadiamsa, first
quarter, in the dasa of the 8th lord”.

Viprakaale phalam sathyam prathyaksham Deva
Keralam which means: The results stated for Vipra
Kaala (i.e. first quarter of a given Nadiamsa) in Deva
Keralam will be experienced in actuality.

These two lines are an interpolation and not
connected with the present Nadiamsa in any manner
and hence secluded.

2770 - 2771. Pisces Ascendant - Suprabha
Nadiamsa:(Upto sloka 2819). One belonging by
birth to the first halfof this Nadiamsa will be a Soodra
and be the third issue of the family. His birth will be
in a city beside a great river.

Notes: Suprabha Nadiamsa, in the sign Pisces is
from 0° 36' to 0° 48'.

2772. Venus: Ifbirth bein the laterhalfas Venus

is in a trine counted from Capricorn, one will be a’

Brahmin and be the 2nd issue.

Notes: The text relates the 3rd lord Venus with
11th house Capricorn. The 3rd rules younger co-born
while the 11th elder. Thus the 3rd lord in an angle to
the 11th house will cause the native not to either
oldest nor the youngest issue. That is he will have
both older and younger co-born. This rule can be
applied to any ascendant.

Angles to the 11th house are: the 2nd, 5th, 8th
and 11th.

2773 - 2774. Rahu:; Should the 4th lord (Mer-
cury) be with Rabu, the street of birth will be spread
from south to north. The house concerned will be
located in the eastern part having a north-facing
entry. It may also correspond to the sign indicated by
the Navamsa occupied by the 4th lord or a trine
thereof.

2775. Jupiter in Aquarius: Ifthe ascendant lord
Jupiter is in the 12th in Aquarius while the 4th lord
Mercury is with a malefic, birth and livelihood will
be in a town near a river.

Chandra Kala Nadi

2776. Coborn: Should Rahu be in the 3rd (Tau-
rus) there will be destruction of coborn. The subject
will have no older coborns and will lose his mother
(early).

2777. One born in the former half of Suprabha
Nadiamsa in a dual sign (here, Pisces) with Scorpio
Navamsa will have no pre-born and after-born (ap-
parently brothers). One sister of his will be endowed
with longevity.

2778. Progeny: One belonging to the later half
of this Nadiamsa will have sons and daughters (i.e.
will have progeny). The one belonging to the former
half will be of mean birth.

Notes: Neechamsa Sambhava = having birth in
a mean family, and does not refer to a planet in
debility in Navamsa and the rendering is accordingly
done although the word Amsa is not an exactly
suitable one here.

2779. Former Half: The subject will have dark
and stout physique. He will be famous in royal service
and will have a famous employment.

2780. Virgo Navamsa: One born in Pisces as-
cendant (between 6 deg 40' and 10 deg 0) with Virgo
Navamsa will live by writing activities, be passionate
and be endowed with a fleet of horses and other
conveyances.

Notes: Here no particular nadiamsa is referred
to, but Pisces ascendant with Virgo Navamsa which
will give writing skills, passion and conveyances of
a rich nature.

2781. Treasurer: One born in the former half of
the subject Nadiamsa will be devoted to Lord Vishnu
and will follow the path prescribed fora Vaishnavite.
He will be a treasurer under an emperor.

2782. Another School of Thought: According
to yet another source, the subject with the said
Nadiamsa in Pisces will beget following effects: His
father will be highly prosperous. So also his paternal
grand-father.

2783. Mother: If the 4th lord Mercury is in the
9th (Scorpio) along with Ketu, onc will have a long-
lived mother. This applies to birth in Suprabha
Nadiamsa in Pisces.

Notes: But for this Nadiamsa, the 4th Jord Mer-
cury inthe 9th ruled by Mars, his bitterenemy will not
prove favourable for mother’s life-span. Moreover
when he joins Ketu in Scorpio, the Pisces native will
not have a long-living mother.

2784. Danger: The native’s fifth year will en-
danger him greatly ifthe 5th lord Moonin Pisces with
Saturn’s aspect or conjunction occupies Scorpio
Navamsa.

2785 - 2786, One born in the subject Nadiamsa
will be predominant with passion, be intelligent,
polite in speech, and be skillful in foreign languages
and acquisition of wealth. He will be intent upon

Deva Keralam 9

serving a king and be found of seeking delight fom
prostitutes.

2787 - 2788, Jupiterian Trimsamsa: Onc born
in Pisces ascendant with Jupiterian Trimsamsa will
be charming in appearance, be fortunate, skillful (in
every respect) and will possess beautiful face and
eyes. He will delight in dance and music and be fond
of scents and garlands. His progress will show up
after 32, more so after 40 years of age.

Notes: The Nadiamsa does not come into play
here. The Trimsamsa of Jupiter rules above 12
degrees but below 20 degrees in Pisces. The ascend-
ant should be anywhere in this space to give above-
mentioned effects.

2789 - 2790, Sister: If the 3rd lord Venus is
associated with an exalted planet, the native will have
a sister who will live in a good abode, but will face
evils in ker childhood. Due to blemishes of the
delivery chamber, the said sister could even die.

Note: It is difficult to say whether the one-line
sloka No. 2790, is actually a part of the current
discussion. This is because of the expression
“Suvaasini’ in sloka 2789 which denotes that the
sister of the native will live ina good abode.

Deva Keralam 11

name of Abhijit Nakshatra be written on the image of
zodiac and be given away to an astrologer. Then the
blemish in respect of progeny will be warded off.

Notes: “a” and “b” are clear.

(c) For Pisces ascendant, the Sth Aroodha will be
so many signs away as the Moonis from Cancer (with
exceptions mentioned by me in my notes in Brihat
Parasara Hora, Part 1, English translation). Mercu-
ry’s position should be insuch place along with Ketu.
Unless Mercury in the yoga obtains Aries navamsa,
obstacles in acquiring progeny should not be ex-
pected. Alternatively Saturn should aspect Pisces
which should ascend between 13° 20' and 16° 40'
getting Scorpio Navamsa. Remedy suggested is do-
nation of a zodiac’s image to an astrologer. The
image should contain inscription of the 28 Nakshatras
including Abhijit.

2804 - 2806. Dasa Effects: (a) In the sub period
of an obstructive (or adversc) planet during the major
rulership of the 8th lord, the subject will lose relatives
of his age group.

(b) The 2nd half of the 4th dasa will cause
him great happiness.

(c) For birth in the former half of the
subject Nadiamsa, see if the 8th lord’s dasa is in
operation. If so, when Jupiter comes to Taurus in
transit, death of mother be declared. So also in the
intervals of Venus and Rahu (without reference to
Jupiter’s transit).

Notes: (a) Obstructive planets for Pisces ascend-
ant are Venus, Mercury and Saturn. The sub period of
one of these planets, or that of occupants thereof will
cause danger to relatives in the main period of Venus.
The Nadiamsa need not be a considerationas the dasa
of 8th lord for any ascendant is capable of causing
grief.

(b) This axiom is a broad clue to this Nadiamsa.

(c) Linking death of mother in 8th lord’s dasa to
the transit of Jupiter applies only to the subject
Nadiamsa.

2807. Lord of the 8th: (a) Should the 8th lord
be inthe 1 1th (i.e. Venus in Capricorn) the native will
be very fortunate. In the end of such 8th lord’s dasa,
his son will fall sick.

(b) The 8th lord Venus obtaining the 4th
place in Navamsa will also give financial acquisition
apart from causing a second marriage.

‘Notes: Two combinations involving Venus are
discussed. (a) By being in the 1 1th house, he will give
fortunes to the native in gencral, and sickness in the
concluding part of Venus Dasa. Venus in the 11th

house for any ascendant except Scorpio is generally
a contributory factor for fortunes. The degree of
fortunes will depend on the circumstances prevailing
in a given case. Sickness to a son is applicable only
to the subject Nadiamsa.

(b) Without reference to Venus in Capri-
corn, his occupying the 4th house from Navamsa
ascendant will give financial gains and second mar-
riage to the native. Venus being a Yoga Karaka, his
occupation of the 4th from Navamsa ascendant will
cause financial acquisition. The issue of second
marriage will get confirmed if the Navamsa ascend-
ant is particularly a dual sign - one of Virgo, Sagitta-
rius and Pisces. Here again, the Nadiamsa may many
times be not relevant.

2808. One born in the former half of Suprabha
Nadiamsa, Pisces will have increased wealth and
coms (eating comforts). In the 4th dasa he will obtain
religious advancement and fame through royal pa-
tronage.

Deva Keralam 13

2820. Kalusha Nadiamsa: Should the Moonbe

placed in Sundari Nadiamsa in aspect to or associa-

-tion with Jupiter, one born in Vipra Kala (of Kalusha
Nadiamsa) will be a Brahmin and be happy.

Notes: Now the text discusses birth in Kalusha
Nadiamsa in an immovable ascendant from sloka
2820 through 2887. When the Moon fetures in the
discussion generally it is prescribed to be Sundari
Nadiamsa (of any sign), unless and until specified.
The text’s authority is Chandra Nadi.

Immovable ascendants are: Taurus, Leo, Scor-
pio and Aquarius. In these signs, Kalusha Nadiamsa
rules from 24° 12' to 24° 28". Its first halfis from 24°
12' to 24° 18' and second half is from 24° 18' to 24°
24! of arc in immovable signs.

The Moon is required to be in Sundari Nadiamsa
ofany sign for which please referto sloka 1584, Book
1 (page 143).

2821 - 28211/2. Infantile Mortality: In case of
birth in Vipra Kala of Kalusha Nadiamsa, the associa-
tion of the Moon with Mars will cause infantile
mortality, while in case of Soodra Kala, there will be
no such adverse effect on account of this conjunc-
tion. Further the native will be ever bappy.

2822 - 28221/2, Instant Death: Birth in an
immovable ascendant with Kalusha Nadiamsa, with
an immovable Navamsa will cause instant death. If
at all be survives, he will be highly prosperous.

2823 - 2825. Umbilical Cord: (a) Rabu in the
9th while the Moon occupies Sundari Nadiamsa will
cause birth entangled in umbilical cord in case of
former half of Kalusha Nadiamsa.

(0) But if birth be in the second half, this
yoga will be ineffective in this regard.

(c) Infantile Mortality: Jupiter’s aspect on
the ascendant incase of birth in former half will offset
effects of infantile mortality. The relief will however"
be less in case of second half of Kalusha Nadiamsa.

2826 - 2827. Leo/Virgo Navamsa: Birth in an
immovable ascendant with Leo/Virgo Navamsa will
cause following effects. The subject will be a Brah-
min, be ever happy and be the third issue of the
family. He will have a fleshy and dark body and will
have noeldercoborn. He will have younger coborns,
be honourable and be happy with his sister.

Notes: Nadiamsa condition should be ignored in
this context, Any one of the three immovable ascend-
aats should rise at birth begetting Leo or Virgo
Navamsa. That is Taurus 23° 20' to 30°; Leo 16° 40’
to 23° 20' or Scorpio 3° 20' to 10° 0' should rise in
which case the Navamsa ascendant will be Leo/
Virgo. (Aquarius will not beget these Navamsas.)
Then the effects given in the text will become appli-
cable.

2828, Kalusha Nadiamsa: The native of an
immovable ascendant, with Leo Navamsaand Kalusha
Nadiamsa will be born in a street spread from east to
south in the southern part (of the town).

14 Chandra Kala Nadi

2829. If birth be in Kalusha Nadiamsa of an
immovable ascendant, the conjunction of Venus -
Mars will destroy the native’s brother while sisters
will not be destroyed.

2830 - 2831. Jupiter in an Angle: For birth in
an immovable ascendant with immovable Navamsa,
see if Jupiter occupies an angle with Cancer Nav-
amsa. If so, the native’s father will be wealthy,
sickly, and will have a dark and emaciated body. He
will experience poverty in childhood and be devoid
of older coborn.

Notes: The Nadiamsa is not relevant. The as-
cendantshould be inanimmovable sign with immov-
able Navamsa (whether Vargottama or not) while
Jupiter is in an angle from the natal ascendant, with
exaltation Navamsa. Needless to mention, the posi-
tion of Jupiter in the sign Aquarius will not grant him
exaltation in Navamsa. So he should be in any other
immovable sign as an angle.

Many good effects about father are discussed
involving Jupiter, who is the 8th lord for Taurus/Leo.
ForScorpio and Aquarius he is the 2nd lord, rulingthe
opposite sign of the 8th. His retrogression is specially
discussed again about father in the ensuing verses but
with the use of subject Nadiamsa.

2832 - 28331/2, Father: For one born in Kalusha
Nadiamsa’s second half, if the 8th lord is retrograde
at birth, following effects about father will come to
pass. He will have a weak body, be gentle, given to

anger, but be instantly pleased, will for some time
take to bad path, will later regret, be troubled by ulcer

and weapons, will be afraid of committing sins, will
honour gods and Brahmins, will follow Vaidic path,
be religious and will worship lord Siva.

2834. Venus ruling the Sth (paternal grand-
father) denotes that the native’s paternal grandfather
will be pure (in character).

Note: This line is a wrong placement here as
Venus does not signify paternal grandfather for any
immovable ascendant. Further, it does not specify
how Venus should be placed. This one line has crept
in here affecting the continuity of effects of slokas
2832-3312 above. the effects now continue in sloka
2835 etc. infra. using retrograde Jupiter.

2835 - 2836, The native’s father will enjoy the
blessings of Lord Siva. He will be endowed with
Satva Guna and be virtuous and will be able to
differentiate betweenSmruti (recorded outof memory)
and Sruthi (directly heard). He will please those who
come to him for help, will have many friends and will
be fond of his kinsmen. (The 2nd line of sloka 2836
has something missing as the planet required to be in
Virgo, to give prosperity to father, is not cited).

2837. Kalusha Nadiamsa: The 3rd dasa will
bring specially more prosperity. The second dasa
will not be so effective (for prosperity). These apply
to birth in second half of this Nadiamsa.

2838 - 2839. One born in  immovable ascend-
ant with Leo Navamsa (or) Kalusha Nadiamsa will

16 Chandra Kala Nadi

for his learning. He will be fond of drama, rhetoric,
poetry and music. He will be an expert in speaking
in Sanskrit and will honour Brahmins and gods.
Notes: Jupiter in the 4th begetting Cancer Nav-
amsa suggests that the birth ascendant should be
either Taurus or Leo or Aquarius, but not Scorpio.

2851 - 2854, Birth in Kalusha Nadiamsa while
the Moon is in Sundari Nadiamsa will cause the
native achieve excellent results in childhood. He will
also be famous in royal service. He will obtain
Rajayoga effects in the 3rd dasa. His father will also
prosper. In the last one third part of the second dasa,
the native will become exceedingly wealthy. During
the currency of the first dasa, when Saturn transits
Cancer, his mother will be endangered. She will have
seen brothers. The subject will have a step-mother.

2855. Jupiter in Sundari Nadiamsa: For a
birth in the second half of Kalusha Nadiamsa (of an
immovable ascendant), if Jupiter occupies Sundari
Nadiamsa, predict marriage in the very first dasa.

Notes: The question of marriage in the very first
should be wisely made depending upon the balance
at birth. Alternatively, the next dasa should be
chosen. The text simply implies an early marriage.

2856 - 2858. Obstacles to Marriage: Venus
should be ina dual sign with Libra Navamsa. The
ascendant should be an immovable one with immov-
able Navamsa for birth in Kalusha Nadiamsa. Then
the native’s marriage will face obstacles. Forbirthin
the same Nadiamsa, Saturn aspecting the 3rd house
will also cause similar obstacles. Marriage will
however occur after two or three weeks of trial. The
bride will have no father at the time of the marriage
of the said native.

2859 - 2862 1/2. By worshipping the astrologer
and donating red-coloured clothes, health will pre-
vail. According to one’s capacity, a golden image of
the Lord Sun be donated after performing prayers and
fire sacrifice (Homa). By worshipping the Sun God,
sins and ignorance and will disappear. For twelve
Sundays, or for 48 (continuous) days, worship of the
Sun God will bring back health. Food should be
avoided for one year (obviously one time fasting is
hinted). Forthree years one should live only on fruits.

Notes: The remedies suggested here have noth-
ing to do in this part of the text as one could
understand. These have been wrongly appended.

2863 - 28631/2, In the sub period of the Sun
during Jupiter’s major period the native will incur

Deva Keralam 17

royal wrath. Afterwards health will prevail (?). The
sub period of the Moon will give both good and bad
effects.

2864 - 2865. Then the native will get married in
his 20th year (overcoming obstacles). For the native
of Kalusha Nadiamsa, the 7th lord joining Venus will
cause heavy rains at the time of marriage.

Notes: The second line of sloka No. 2864 given
above should have appeared immediately after sloka
2858, as slokas 2859 - 28621/2 are not a part hereof.

2866 - 28661/2, Spouse: Jupiter begetting the
lordship of 7th Aroodha pada, being in aspect to
Saturn and obtaining Cancer Navamsa will give a
dark-bodied spouse, and happiness after marriage.

2867 - 2869. Father: For 016 born in the latter
part of Kalusha Nadiamsa (in an immovable sign)
father’s death will occur within one-and-a-half year

Notes: The expression “Saayanaabde” may be
interpreted by some as the year 27. This does not

‘apply to logic as “Ayana” is not part of Katapayadi

2870. The subject of second half of Kalusha
Nadiamsa having a malefic yoga (in the ascendant)

2871 - 2872. Should the 7th lord be in aspect to
Saturn, he will join women of many castes. Venus
aspected by Satur will make the native join many
women. After some time he will emerge pure-
hearted, will become famous in royal service, will

2873 - 2874, Paternal Uncle: Jupiter ruling the
11th house (ascendant being Taurus or Aquarius, an
immovable sign) if aspected by Saturn and begets

suffer penury during his childhood and will enjoy
happiness in the second half of his life.

2875.Family Division: During the last one third
part ofsecond dasa, the subject of Kalusha Nadiamsa
(immovable sign) will incur a division with his

Deva Keralam 19

2888 - 2892. Kanthaa Nadiamsa - Pisces As-
cendant: (a) One born in this part of the zodiac with
Saturn in Scorpio (in the 9th house) will lose his
father in childhood and will be bereft of after- born.
He will incur grief due to loss of elder brother in the
middle of his life. He will have two sisters, out of
whom one will be long-lived, endowed with sons and
happiness. He will obtain happiness in early days by
serving a king. Afterwards he will go to forests (or
will migrate) and will serve a mean king. He will
have two wives and good (male) children.

(b) Due to Amala Yoga (caused by a benefic
being placed in the 10th in Sagittarius), he will obtain
men-borne conveyances by performing rituals as
prescribed in the Vedas.

(c) In the 4th dasa, he will be greatly happy. In
the middle part thereof, he will earn wealth. In the
second half of the said dasa, he will beget convey-
ance and villages (i.e. some properties or position of
eminence). (Dhaatamse is a wrong addition in sloka
2892. Dhaatamsa is cited as Ghaatamsa in some
parts of the text.)

Notes: (a) The above results for Saturn’s posi-
tion in Scorpio should apply only when Kanthaa
Nadiamsa rises in Pisces. Otherwise Satum in the 9th
for Pisces will just give troubles to father, and a
second marriage to the native (11th lord aspecting the
11th house, is a slow moving planet.

(b) Amala yoga giving conveyance can be ap-
plied to any part of Pisces, and also to other ascend-
ants.

(c) The effects cited here should be read de-

linking Saturn in the 9th, and also Amala Yoga.
These just relate to Kanthaa nadiamsa, Pisces as-
cendant. Additional effects are cited in slokas 2897-
98.

Kanthaa Nadiamsa occurs twice in each sign.

Movable sign: once from 6° to 6° 12’, and again
from 10° to 10° 12',

Immovable sign: once from 23° 48' to 24° 0', and
again from 19° 48' to 20° 0'.

Common sign: once from 25° to 25° 12', and
again from 21° to 21° 12.

2893. Saturn: If Saturn is found in the

wife (as well), be honourable, and be famous in royal

Note: Slokas No. 2893 -6 are wrongly appended
in this part of the text, and should be carefully and
profitably used without mixup.

2894 - 2896 Jupiter: Should Jupiter be in
‘Pareswara’ (should be Parameswari) Nadiamsa,
while Saturn is in Ghaata Nadiamsa, the subject will’
be fortunate and be devoted to Lord Vishnu and Lord
Siva. After the age of 38, he will be fortunate, and

effects. ,

Notes: Neither Ghaata nor Dhaata Nadiamsa is

Nadiamsa will face obstacles to his education. In the

Note: This sloka does not give full clues and is

2900 - 29001/2, Movable ascendant: For a birth

in Kantha Nadiamsa in movable ascendant (specifi-

cally Cancer), if Mercury occupies Somavathi

Nadiamsa in Virgo in the company of the Sun, the

Notes: From sloka 2900 to 2912, the text deals

For Kantha Nadiamsa, see notes given under

And the next chapter from sloka 2913 also deals

“Somasuthamse” should read as “Somalatamse”

meaning: "in Somalatha Nadiamsa” the same as
Somavathi Nadiamsa which in Virgo rules from 3° 0'
to 3° 12'. The planet Mercury should be in this space
in the company of the Sun (whose degree is unspeci-
fied) while the ascendant is in Cancer in Kanthaa
nadiamsa. As a result, the subject will be the first
issue without an older coborn.

2901 - 29021/2, Should Venus be in an angle or

Nadiamsa ina movable ascendant will be a Brahmin,
be ever happy and be the eldest issue of the family.
The house of birth will correspond to the Rasi or its
trine with reference to the Navamsa occupied by 4th
lord.

Notes: Venus is the 1 1thlord for Cancerascend-
ant and hence his position is discussed in the context

2905. Jupiter in Pisces Navamsa: The native of
former half of the subject Nadiamsa, in a movable
ascendant, having Jupiter in Pisces Navamsa will be

calling.
Notes: Jupiter is the lord of the 9th for Cancer

ascendant. His occupation of Pisces Navamsa will

2906. Kanthaa Nadiamsa - First half: This
native of a movable ascendant will be devoted to
Lord Vishnu and follow the path prescribed for the
Vaishnavites. He will befriend Brahmins in the
assembly of an emperor.

2907 - 2908. The native of second half of this
Nadiamsa will prosper in the third dasa. One belong-
ing to the former half will havea long living mother,
if Venus ruling the 4th house occupies Libra itself,
with Aries Navamsa. But if the birth be in the 2nd
half, death of mother will occur in the very childhood
of the native.

Notes: For Cancer ascendant Venus in the 4th in

His position simultaneously in Aries Navamsa will

half of Kanthaa Nadiamsa, in Cancer ascendant,
while the same position will cause loss of mother in
childhood in case of birth in second half of the
Nadiamsa in Cancer ascendant.

Thus Venus in Aries navamsa, being in the sign
Libra, implies some danger to mother in general.

2909 - 29091/2, Mars in Sth: For Cancerascend-
ant, Kanthaa Nadiamsa, if Mars ruling the 5th house

amsa position, the subject will be greatly endangered

in the Sth year. Additional effects thereof will be:
danger to mother, and acquisition of a step-mother
(i.e. another marriage for father).

Notes: For Cancer ascendant, Mars in the 2nd
(Leo), Sth (Scorpio) or 8th (Aquarius), getting Sag-
ittarius Navamsa is discussed above. Thus Mars
should be in one of the following spaces for a native
of Cancer:

1. Leo 26° 40' to 30° 00'

2. Sco 16° 40' to 20° 00'

3, Aqu 06° 40' to 10° 00"

2910 - 2912. Father & Paternal Uncle: (a)

(c) Incase ofbirthin former half of this Nadiamsa,

(b) and (c) - Applicable only to the subject
Nadiamsa.

Nadiamsa: The native of Cancer Ascendant with

luous voice, be intelligent, and devoted to serving the

2960 - 2961. Champaka Nadiamsa - First
Half: The native of this part of the zodiac with the
Moon in Indra Nadiamsa will be beautiful. intelli-

2981-2985. Dasa Effects - Libra Ascendant:
(a) Should the dasa lord be devoid of strength, one
will be deprived of splendour (i.e. he will turn pale,
without much blood). Through a benefic planet, only
benefic effects will come to pass; how come there
will be no happiness through it?

(b) If the dasa lord is conjunct the Navamsa
dispositor of a Yoga Karaka planet, the subject will
gain housing properties and the like. He will further
acquire spouse, progeny and friends and royal recog-
nition.

(c) If the dasa lord is placed ina Navamsa of an
inimical planet, then in his dasa, in the sub period of
(another) malefic, there will be fear from thieves and
enemies, loss of position or displacement, enmity
with elders in the family and obstacles in performing
royal duties.

(d) If the dasa lord is weak, is placed in the 8th
and is related to 8th lord by aspect or association, one
will incur damage to his calling and money apart
from developing enmity with coborn. The lord of a
dasa placed at birth ina Rasi/Navamsa of an inimical
planet will in the sub period of his relevant dispositor
promote expenditure on account of enemies.

. 2986 - 2988. Libra Ascendant - Jupiter: (a) If
Jupiter is in Pisces at birth, the subject of Libra
ascendant will be dear to his king, be religious,

learned in meanings of Sastras, passionate and eve
peaceful in disposition.

(b) One will be dear to king if Jupiter occupies
the 10th in Navamsa, or be in a friendly Navamsa
(while in the sign Pisces).

(c) IfJupiter, placed in Scorpio, begets Mercurian
or Venusian Navamsa (Virgo/Libra), he will do acts

(1) Jupiter in the 10th with Libra Navamsa will

Notes: Instructions contained in “a” to “f” are
quite clear and are in line with generally available
information. These can be applied as a common
yard-stick.

Instructions in “g” are not clear. Apparently the
text wants to specify something about Sth lord’s dasa.
What all we could gather is that Bhinna and Prastara
Ashtaka Vargas should be used (as per rules laid
down in general).

3005. The importance of the Moon & Ascend-
ant: The Moon is the body while the ascendant (i.e.
ascending degree) is its breath (or spirit or vitality).
After an examination of both these elements, effects
of all the Nadiamsas be declared.

3006. The Ashtaka Vargas of the Moon and the
lord of ascendant be examined while analysing the
effects of those dasas.

Notes: The words “Taddasaayaam” may other-
wise mean the dasas of the Moon and the lord of
ascendant. Yet it is more logical to depend on these
Ashtaka Vargas universally in respect of all dasas
generally in view of sloka 3005 above.

3007. Libra ascendant - Pisces Navamsa: One
born in this part with Mudgara Nadiamsa will be

prosperous and happy in the dasa represented by the
Nakshatra denoted by processing the Ashtaka Varga
of the Moon.

Notes: In the first instance, Mudgara Nadiamsa,
be ignored for itis a corrupt addition. We should take
only Pisces Navamsa to be valid as ascending as part
of Libra ascendant.

Secondly the clue given for analysing the dasa
with the help of Moon’s Ashtaka Varga can be
applied to any ascendant, not just to Libra with Pisces
Navamsa. The meaning is explained below:

The use of the Moon’s Ashtaka Varga is ex-
plained ina brief manner by the text without referring
to a specific house. The word “Avasisita” meaning
remainder tells us of the involvement some process
of division. With the help of know instructions, we
can understand the same thus:

There will be Bindus in the various signs, even
as low as zero, in the Moon’s Ashtaka Varga. Since
prosperity is referred to, we can take the Bindus inthe
pre-rectified Ashtaka Varga chart prepared for the
Moon. Her Sodya Pinda be multiplied by the said
numberof Bindus and dividend by 27. The remainder
will represent a Nakshatra counted from Aswini
which will yield a certain planet’s Vimsottari dasa.
This dasa will bring prosperity.

For example there are five bindus in the 9th
(counted from natal ascendant) inthe Moon’s Ashtaka
Varga, and her Sodya Pinda is, say, 80. Thus 5
multiplied by 80 and divided by 27 leaves a remain-
der of 25, which represents Poorvabhadrapada
Nakshatra. Thus, Jupiter’s dasa in this case will give
prosperity.

and will possess somewhat a diseased physique.

Three planets play a leading role in destroying
one’s happiness.

1) The Navamsa dispositor of Saturn.

2) The Navamsa dispositor of Mandi. [ Mandi and
Gulika are one and the same.]

3) The Navamsa dispositor of the planet in whose
sign the 8th lord is places. Example - Pisces ascend-

Leo. The dispositor of Venus is the Sun. The Sun is
in the Navamsa say of Mercury. Hence it is Mercury
that can spoil happiness in this case.

For exact calculation of Mandi, see sloka 70, ch.3
of Brihat Parasara Hora, part 1, English translation
by R. Santhanam. The position of Mandi is to be
calculated for the beginning Muhurta of Saturn, and
not ending Muhurta, as further justified in the exam-

ibid.

3050. Jupiter in Retrogression: The effects due

heart. Rectal and urinary disorders are also part of
Venus in the 7th linked to Saturn by aspect or

1. I, Venkatesa, belonging to Kasyapa 0018,
respectfully salute Lord Venkateswara, the Glori-
ous, and narrate effects (of astrological combina-
tions) for the (pleasure of the) king.

Notes: For the part from sloka 1 above till the
end, i.e. sloka No.4775 appearing hereafter, the
author's name is givenas Venkatesa. The name of the
author in the earlier portion is noted as Achyuta. We
do not know whether it is the same author with a
different name, or an entirely different author. But
the language used in the original seems to be from one
and the same person.

In the present context, the 0074 of the author is
given as Kasyapa and hence he was a descendant of
sage Kasyapa.

[Kasyapa was a mythical sage, husband of Aditi
and twelve other daughters of Daksha. By Aditi, he
was father of Adityas and of Maha Vishnu. By his
other twelve wives, he was father of demons, Nagas,
reptiles, birds and all kinds of living things. He was
one of the seven Great Rishis (Sapta Rishis) and priest
of Parasu Rama and Lord Sri Rama Chandra.]

The author Venkatesa was apparently enthused
by some royal patronage to compile the present work.
He should not be confuséd with Sarvartha
Chintamani's author, Venkatesa Daivagna, who in
fact belonged to Bharadwaja Gotra.

Lord Venkatesa invoked by our author is an
incarnation of Lord Vishnu and resides on Spataginis
the Seven Hills, in Tirupati

This relates to computation of Jaimini Dasa and
is a general yardstick for the various kinds of special
Dasas enunciated by the sage. For odd signs, viz.
Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius and Aquarius
the counting is in the straight ascending order. For
even signs, viz. Taurus, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio,
Capricorn and Pisces, the counting is to be reversed.
This method of counting, that is in straight order for
odd signs and in reverse order for even signs, is called
Prakruti Chakra. ;

Sometimes a converse method is to be adopted.
To wit, the counting is in the reverse order in case of
odd signs, and in straight order in case of even signs.
This is called Vikruti Chakra.

Whether odd sign or even sign, the counting is
universally in straight order. This is called Audayika
Chakra.

The 4th one is Graha Chakra, This is to be
counted, in a straight manner as per the rules pre-
scribed, from the sign occupied by the lord of the
ascendant. The next planet in the Rasi chartis the 2nd
Bhava, its next is the 3rd Bhava. This way after
exhausting the 9 Bhavas with 9 planets, the lord of
the ascendant himself becomes the 10th bhava, the
2nd again becomes the 11th bhava, and the sixth
becomes the 12th Bhava.

From the Rasi of the Dasa concerned, the number
of signs counted up to the sign occupied by the lord
of the Dasa Rasi, minus one, will be the number of
years of the dasa. The Dasa years will never exceed
12. If the dasa lord is in the 7th from the dasa Rasi,
consider 10 years, The dasa years divided by twelve
will yield the durations of the various Buktis. Ac-
cording the Rasi being odd or even, the distribution
should be in direct or reverse order.

10 -22. Longevity Computation: One's longev-
ity should be found based on:

(a) lords of ascendant and the 8th;

(b) ascendant and the Moon;

(c) ascendant and Hora ascendant.

Deva Keralam 77

its Aroodha. In case of odd signs the counting is from
that very sign. If even sign is involved, the counting
is from the opposite sign.

Notes: In these slokas, firstly the well-known
principle of life span being long, medium or short is
incompletely conveyed in the first instance. In the
second instance calculation of Savayana Hora Lagna
is prescribed.

(a) Use of lords of ascendant and 8th based on
Jaimini's Sutra "Aayuh pitru dinesaabhyaam": If
both these lords are in movable sign/s, long life is
indicated. If one is ina fixed sign while the other is
in a dual sign, then.again full life span will come to
pass. Medium life span will result if one is in a
movable sign and the other is in an immovable sign.
Short life is denoted by both these planets being in
immovable signs. The same is the case when one of
them is ina movable sign and the other ina dual sign.

(b) Use of ascendant and Moon sign should also
be done with the same guidelines, replacing the pair
of planets at "a".

(c) Again with the help of Janma Lagna (natal
ascendant) and Hora Lagna, similar assessment be
made.

If the indications as per the three methods are
identical, itshould be accepted as final. Should there
be contradictions, the indication given by the pair of
Janma Lagna and Hora Lagna is alone final.

(There is a school of thought that if two pairs
yield identical hints, that alone be considered.)

The method of Hora Lagna calculation given by
our text is used to find out what is called Saavayava
Hora Lagna.

Other matters discussed in the slokas are clear.

25. The said Matta Maatanga Yoga will be

26. Malaviya Yoga: Venus in strength in an
angle identical with his own sign or signof exaltation
will cause what is called Malaviya Yoga. The person
born in this yoga will be the lord of a number of
elephants, horses, chariots and palanquins, apart
from lands, forts and the like. He will be an ocean of
numerous virtues, be endowed with an excellent
wife, attached to other women, will win over the lord
of men (i.e. the king) and will enjoy pleasures.

Notes: Malaviya yoga is formed in the following
ways.

Venus in Pisces in case of Gemini, Virgo, Sag-
ittarius and Pisces ascendants (dual signs).

Venus in Libra in case of Aries, Cancer, Libra
and Capricorn ascendants (movable signs).

Venus in Taurus in case of Taurus, Leo, Scorpio
and Aquarius ascendants (immovable signs).

Thus all the 12 ascendants enjoy this yoga with
Venus in a specified place.

It comes to our notice, in the course of our
studies, that Venus in debilitation Navamsa is greatly
crippled of its possible superior role through Malaviya
Yoga. So is again the case if he joins Mercury closely
in Pisces, oris combust in any of the signs concerned.

Incase of the yoga formed by exalted Venus, his
dispositor, Jupiter, should not be in the company of
Saturn. If that be the case, the person will be in the
receiving end, instead of enjoying Maha Purusha
Yoga effects. (Refer to sloka 27 infra.)

simultaneous exaltation, vide 4th line in the original
sloka 31 above. It is clear that these lines have
wrongly been appended in the present place. Hence
we can put the results as under in one place for easy
understanding.

Four planets simu|tancously exalted cause ab-
ject poverty, loss of honour, difficulties, miserliness,
and proneness to discascs apart from the only good
effect, viz. courageous disposition.

33 - 4. Adhi Yoga: In the formation of
Chandradhi Yoga, should the Moon be endowed with
full rays, the person concerned will be the ruler of the
earth, be strong, will enjoy pleasures, will own
wealth, elephants etc. With benefics in the 7th, 6th
or(and) 8th from the Moon (orascendant, as “Jasmin”

reads as “Lagnam” in another version), Adhi Yoga is
formed. As a result one will be an army chief,

minister or king and be endowed with wealth, com-
forts, might and vanquished enemies. He will be
long-lived and will be free from fear of illness.

Notes: The text refers only to Chandradhi Yoga.
Lagnadhiyoga being equally important and similar,
suitable addition in brackets is made by me.

Normally, commentators interpreted that
benefics (that is the three natural ones) in the 7th
produce an army chief, in the 6th - a minister, and in
the 8th - a king. One way they may be right. Then
the inference is that the 81h having the three benefics
produces the best Adhi yoga followed by 6th and 7th,
in that order.

Whether itis Lagnadhiyoga or Chandradhiyoga,
the Moon should have undiminished rays. In the first
line of sloka 33, we find the term “Poornamsabhaage”.
Thave not tried to enrich the interpretation with some
new meanings. Removing redundant possibilities, I
have given a practical interpretation.

Also see sloka 36 and others infra.

35. If the luminaries are found, along with all

are confined to the 9th, the subject will be kingly,

("Antarbhava" reads as "Antarbhaye" in another

elephants.

36. Whetherin Lagnadhi Yoga, orin Chandradhi

question goes in vain.

Notes: A benefic additionally mentioned for the
5th house position denotes that the text does not need
all the three natural benefics, viz. Jupiter, Venus and
Mercury, to be in the 6th, 7th and 8th. In my opinion

80 Chandra Kala Nadi

this yoga is formed by all the 3 benefics in the
specified houses, when the question of a benefic ina

According to Horasara, the fourth from the

against the 5th house by Deva Keralam.

37. One born in Chandradhi Yoga will be an

Notes: From this we understand that three

Medium life-span and many sons come in the picture

But the life span will be short.

Notes: Here again, as in the previous aphorism,

40. Saturn - Venus yoga: If the planet Saturn in

43. If all the planets occupy variously their own

time of birth), or be in Vargottama Navamsa, they
will cause auspiciousness and happiness in their
(dasas and) sub periods.

Notes: Deeptavastha: A planet in his exaltation
Sign attains this Avastla or state. (Deeptha = blaz-
ing.)

Muditavastha: Being in a close friend’s sign.
Mudita denotes happiness; also known as Pramudita.

These two Avasthas or states are among nine
suchstates relating to the positions of various planets.

Deva Keralam 81

Temporary strength = Strong in Shadbala or six

(temporal strength inclusive of Ayana Bala), Cheshta
Bala (motional strength), Naisargika Bala (natural

48 - 51. Chaamara Yoga: (a) The lord of the 9th
in the company of the lord of the 4th and being in
exaltation while Venus is in the 5th from the ascend-
ant, will cause Chaamara Yoga. The native of Aries
ascendant with this Yoga will enjoy prosperity (in
general) (and more so) after the age of sixteen. He
will be a king, or be rich and happy.

(b) In case of Chaamara Yoga occurring to
Cancer or Aries ascendant, with Venus in an angle or
a trine, there will be a “Raja Yoga”. The person
belongingto Chaamara Yoga willenjoy Yoga effects
fromthe age of 12. Butthe twelfth year willusher fear
from untimely death. If he survives this danger, long
life is assured.

Notes: (a) According to our text, the 9th lord
should be exalted and be with the lord of the 4th.
Venus should also be in the 5th from ascendant. Then
the yoga in question forms.

Two more kinds of yoga with this name is
explained by Parasara thus.

First, the ascendant lord should be exalted in an
angle and be in aspect to Jupiter. Saturn, Mars, and
Mercury can be exalted this way; that is the ascend-
ants concerned, according to this axiom, could be
Aries, Gemini, Virgo and Capricorn.

Parasara’s second type of yoga is two benefics
jointly should be in the ascendant, 7th, 9th or 10th.
The effects for either kind of Chaamara Yoga are:
royal status, royal honour, long life, scholarship,
eloquence and high rate of knowledge in all arts.

Mantreswara gives yet another situation for this
yoga. The ascendant should contain a benefic while
its lord be ina good house (Bhava) orin his exaltation
sign. The effects are long life, increasing prosperity,
fame, virtues and leadership.

Thus the Chaamara yoga is described in four
different ways by different authorities.

Adhamaadhama (the meanest) are produced (when

70-71. Maha Panditha Yoga (Yoga for Great
Scholarship): If the Sun and Mercury join ina sign
which is the ascendant and is the place of exaltation
for one of them, or if Jupiter (alone) is in the
ascendantin exaltation, the yoga called Maha Panditha
Yoga is produced. As a result, the person will be a
scholar, ruler of men and land, wealthy, will be
scientific (ormaintaina scriptural viewpoint), learned,
be a good speaker in the assembly of Brahmins, a
protector of secrets, and be wise and virtuous.

Notes: This yoga forms in three ways.

Firstly, the Sun and Mercury can be in Aries
ascendant, where the Sun is exalted.

Secondly, the Sun and Mercury can be in Virgo
ascendant, where Mercury is exalted.

Thirdly, Jupiter can be alone in Cancer rising.

95. Evil Lords: The 8th lord in the 6th or 12th,
the 6th lord in the 8th or 12th, and the 12th lord in the
6th or 8th, or these three planets in their above-
specified own houses in mutual aspects only between
them (to the exclusion of other association) - this
yoga will make one a king, endowed with distinct
wealth and be praised by the king of kings.

Notes: The yoga simply requires evil lords in
evil houses.

In this context, Uttara Kalamrita’s much popu-
lar Vipareeta Raja Yoga is to be noted.

Lord of 8th in 6th or 12th, lord of 6th in 8th or
12th, or lord of 12th in 6th or 8th or the said three evil
lords connected in any way by exchange, aspect or
conjunction, and simultaneously having no such
relationship with other planets - produces Vipareeta
Raja Yoga. The formation of this yoga is not that
simple; so also its effects. The person concemed will
be “the king of kings”. He will have fame and wealth.

Mahadeva in his Jataka Tatva lays down a
simple rule to the effect that the 8th lord and the 6th
lord interchanging places, or being angular, produce
a mighty and powerful person. This aphorism of
Mahadeva is somewhat akin to sloka 96 infra.

104. Should the lord of the 1st house be in
exaltation or be in Vargottama Navamsa, and be in

90 Chandra Kala Nadi

Notes: Advaita Philosophy = Identity of Brahma
(supreme soul) with Jeevatman (human soul).

Vedantha = complete knowledge of the Vedas,
the second and the most important part of Mimamsa,
or third of the three great divisions of Hindu philoso-
phy.

An average reader not properly initiated into
astrology may run the risk of involving the planet
Venus in a lower level of material enjoyments. The
higher plane of Venus should never be missed, as he
is noted to be the able preceptor of demons knowing
many things that were not known even to Deva Guru
or Jupiter. Venus or Bhrigu is said to be the exclusive
knower of imparting life into the dead, known as
Mrita Sanjivani Mantra. He is an unparalleled expert
in Muburta.

pect, destruction of wealth will come to pass. Further,
the person will be separated from his coborn. Should
the dispositor of the lord ofa dasa be inenemy’s Rasi/
Navamsa, there will be fear and distress on account
of enemies. The sub period concerned will be of that
planet which is inimical to the lord of the dasa, or of
a planet that is in its own sign.

432. Jupiter in Pisces: One having Jupiter in
Pisces will be liked by his king, be righteous, ac-
quainted with higher knowledge, passionate, peace-
ful and virtuous.

Notes: As per Kalyana Varma, Jupiter in Pisces
will give expertise in ancient Sastras. The subject
will be honoured by friends and virtuous men. He will
enjoy a leading position under the king’s employ,
will be wealthy, will not incur defeat, and be skillful
in policy matters. His undertakings will prosper.

433. Jupiter in the 10th House: (a) Ifthe planet
Jupiter is found in he 10th house, one will be a
minister.

(b) Jupiter in the 10th with a friend’s Navamsa

Deva Keralam 125

will make one dear to king.

(c) Ifthe Navamsa in question is ruled by Mer-
cury or Venus, Jupiter in the 10th will involve the
subject in trouble and will make him perform such
acts which will arouse the wrath of the ruler.

Notes: (a) This position of Jupiter in the 10th
refers to-no specific Navamsa. The text tersely
indicates a superior professional position for the
subject.

Jupiter in the 10th will further give wealth,
welfare and happiness in general, conveyances, fa-
vourable kinsfolk and fame. Whatever be the under-
taking, the subject will complete it successfully. He
will be disposed to much eating. His sexual
enjoyments will be limited. He will be short-tem-
pered and his contributions to the society will last
long. He will obtain patronage of influential men.
Richness is a part of this position of Jupiter. The
subject will be learned in many Sastras including The
Bhagavad Gita.

There are authorities to state that Jupiter in the
10th carries some inberent blemish to cause worries
about progeny.

Also see sloka 436 infra.

(b) If Jupiter in the 10th occupies a Navamsa
owned by the Sun, the Moon or Mars, the person will
be close to high-ups in power.

(c) In this context, Jupiter begetting Gemini,
Virgo, Libra or Taurus Navamsa, will cause profes-
sional difficulties from powerful people.

plenty of sinful acts; he will instantly befriend those

that are inimical to his ruler, will be inimical to his '

maternal relatives, and will have a secretively de-

436. Jupiter in the 10th House: The person
having Jupiter in the 10th house will be blessed with
much wealth, the nine kinds of precious stones, and
large golden chains (or ornamenis); he will be an

business activities ofall kinds and spiritual Initiation.
He will be firm in disposition, be honoured by a
superior king, and be himself an ocean of virtues and
good deeds. However he will incur enmity with his
kinsfolk.

Notes: The results mentioned in this sloka and

437. Great Prosperity: Shouid the Moon join

438. Jupiter in Libra Navamsa: Placed in

amsa. Asa result one will be close to his maternal
aunt’s children but be inimical to his paternal uncle.

Notes: "Swarajasya Suta Trayam" denoting three

prosperity, drought in the country, fear from enemies
and later on health.
This may be ignored.

461 - 463, Saturn in Svathi: When Saturn
transits Svathi, there will be destruction of enemies,
and incurring fear. One will befriend enemies from
the time the Sun moves in Capricorn through Pisces
(coinciding with Saturn’s stay inSvathi). Should the
Sun, then, be found in transit in the signs commenc-
ing from Aries, one’s enemics will be destroyed.
However with the Sun in Gemini, there will be some
distress and later on health will prevail. With the
Sun’s change of course in Pisces, one will attain
success in undertakings while happiness will prevail
with the change of Sun’s course in Cancer. In
between there will be distress.

Notes: Sloka 463 has an incompatible term
“Meenayana”. The Sun does not change his course
in Pisces. Hence this is a wrong term. His transit in
Meena or Pisces is part of his northerly course or
Uttarayana. ;

For ready information of the reader, the Sun
changes his course twice in a year. When he enters
Sayana (tropical) Capricorn, he commences his north-
erly course, i.e. Uttarayana. When he enters Sayana
Cancer, he commences his southerly course,
Dakshinayana.

For Hindus' religious purposes, these courses are
detected on Nirayana (sidercal) basis.

464. Saturn in Visakha: The planet Saturn
moving in the Nakshatra Visakha will enable the
subject befriend enemies and enjoy great happiness
in general.

willbe in the grip of fear. In the fourth Nakshatra, the
transit of Saturn will cause distress and quarrels in
native country, trouble to populace (?), mental wor-
ries and enmity with the king of Pandya country. This
transit will also cause the native’s destruction. The
2ndand 4th Nakshairas being in transit by Saturn will

When malefics transit natal ascendant, 10th house
and Adhana ascendant, the subject will incur

illness to self, and troubles to his kinsfolk. Ifthese are
in transit by bencfic plancts, auspicious results will
come to pass, and if by malefics, mental distress will
be incurred. The wise should deduce the effects
according to caste, community ete.

Notes: Also see slokas 482 - 485 infra.

In all the cases of transits ona given Nakshatra,
the 10th and 19th thereof will also pass similar
effects. Final effects will depend on the age of the
native concerned.

494 - 496, The wise should deduce the following
fromthe 12 houses of a horoscope and declare effects
accordingly.

The ascendant - Fame, physique, figure and
disposition.

2nd house - Gold (i.e.wealth), eyes, family, face
and food.

3rd house - Ability in war, prowess, cheek and
ears.

4th house - chest, comforts, residence, convey-
ances and pleasures. (“Mukha” should be read as
“Sukha” to mean comforts. “Mukha” means mouth
which is ruled by the 2nd house and not by the 4th
house.)

Sth house: Stomachial region, mind, sons, hand
(?) and disciples.

6th house: Step-mother, enemies, ulcers, dis-
ease, poison, navel.

7th house: Wife, the lower belly or pelvis, urine,
sexual desire, amorous passion, sexual enjoyment.

8th house: Hole (Randhra itself means the 8th
house), calumny, death, lack of cleanliness, secretive
diseases.

9th house: Fortunes, righteousness, gods, auspi-
cious acts, prosperity, preceptors, and penance.

10th house: Honour, foreign residence, Yagnas
(Vedic rituals), profession, fame, self-conceit, au-
thority and success.

1] thhouse: Attainment without obstacles, wealth
(or exalted position) and well-being.

12th house: Obstacles, loss of wealth, destruc-
tion and bed-comforts.

Notes: As for the8th house, which is called
Randhrasthaana, hole means the cavity through which

the life force leaves the body at the time of death.
There are nine holes in the body. Some recognise 10,
including the cavity at skull. That is why the 8th is
related to end of life. The ancients believed thatwhen
the life force left the body through the hole of theskull
- called Brahma Randhra - one attained Sathya Loka,
the world of Brahma and got rid of further births.
Should the life force leave the body through the hole
of rectal orurinary passage, the dead would go to hell
and would have rebirths.

560. The number of Bindus in Venus’ Ashtaka

Should the 7th lord (from the ascendant) be exalted,

Notes: To geta proper meaning of this sloka, we
have to supply certain information ourselves. In-
volvement of 3rd lord Venus, 7th lord Saturn exalted
in the 3rd, and reference to long life - all these point
out to two things:

1. The ascendant discussed is Leo.

Notes: Mudgara Nadiamsa extends thus: from

“Madhyame Kale” vide sloka 881 denotes the

gives her Vargottama position in Libra.

925 - 937. Ona day of Ashtami, Chaturdasi,

Chirajiva Nadiamsa extends thus: from 12° 24'

941 - 943, Chirajiva Nadiamsa - Former Half:

Notes: “Mahakaala Gurudvare Sarva-

stroyer". Thus, the native will hold a position witha

subject will be engaged in spreading Lord Siva’s
school of thought, distinctly opposed to Vaishnavism.

For those who want freedom to interpret this
complicated expression, the other meanings of
Mahakaala are supplied below.

Mahakaala = A place sacred to the destroyer-
Siva; a mythical mountain; a teacher so named;
Durga in Her terrific form; one of Her attendants; one
of the sixteen Vidya-devis; the Linga in Ujjayini; a
Goddess who executed the commands of fifth Arhat
of the present Avasarpini.

Loss of army chief and minister will come to pass

Notes: Madhvacharya was the founder of a sect
of Vaishnavites in the south of India. He was a
Kanarese Brahmin called Ananda Tirtha
Bhagavthpada, or Madhu, said to have been born
about 1200 A.D. His doctrine is commonly called
Dvaita - i. e. “Duality” - (in opposition to the
“Advaita”, i.e. Non-duality of the great Vedanthist
Sankaracharya) and his sectarians are called Madhvas.

Notes: (0) Shashtiamsa rulership is very simple

1814. Jupiter in 9th: The dasa of the 9th lord
(Mercury) will cause financial gains through royal
ministers’ mercy provided Jupiter is in the 9th
(Gemini).

Notes: In “b” at sloka 1812-1813, Mercury dasa
is said to be auspicious in case of Libra ascendant as
for marriage of the native’s son. Further grand-
children will be born to the subject according to his
age.

But in the current sloka, an essential condition is
laid down for Mercury dasa to be favourable to the
subjectin terms of financial gains - that Jupitershould
be in the 9th at birth. This means with Jupiter in the
9th, the Libra native’s Mercury dasa will not be so
helpful for progress.

Jupiter is one of the most adverse planets for
Libra ascendant. Mercury is also the lord of the 1216
though simultaneously of 9th, and can cause some
mischief in his dasa at some stage or the other. To
counter his partial evil propensities, anequally tough
evil lord is needed. And that is none other than
Jupiter.

This kind of hidden clues in the Nadi texts stand
apart and prove quite helpful in analysis of a practical
horoscope.

Insloka 1816-1817, forexample, Mercuryas the
12th lord is said to be dangerous forthe progeny of the
native in his own dasa and own sub period. The text
does not call Mercury the 9th lord in that context.
This shows how a “favourable planet” can also be
unfavourable.
